meta.title: Nagila Decastro
meta.description: Mother of two girls, wife, homeschool educator, and mentor to mothers. Sleep consulting, real motherhood, homeschool, and METI Mentorship.

quote.text: The home cared for first holds everything else up.

body: I am Nagila — mother to Helena and Catharina, wife to Ewerton, living on Cape Cod. Every birth rewrote what I understood about rest, presence and home.
I learned firsthand that a baby's sleep is not a technical detail — it is the oxygen of an entire family. When a mother sleeps again, she returns to herself.

I have homeschooled my two daughters from day one. Not as a rejection of the world, but as a deep yes to time — the time that does not come twice, the time it takes to pass character on at the child's own pace.
Today I walk alongside mothers and families who want more than to survive motherhood and parenting. Families who want to live inside it all with clarity, ease and purpose.
Helena arrived in 2021. Catharina in 2024. Ewerton in 2017.
Before any mother I walk alongside, there is this home. It is the home I speak from, the one I return to, the place where everything I teach was first lived.

services.sectionSubtitle: Four mentorship fronts, one root: caring for the mother and the home is caring for everything that comes after.

cards.description: A human, personalized plan for your baby's sleep — honoring the bond, the child's temperament and your family's real rhythm. No harsh formulas, no guilt.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from your site — I'd like to know more about Sleep Consulting

detail.intro: If you're here, sleep is not happening. Maybe it never has. Maybe it did for a while and then regressed. Maybe you can't even remember the last full night you slept — and you're exhausted enough that you don't know what to try anymore.
whyBody: A baby's sleep is not a technical detail. It is the oxygen of the family. Sleep-deprived mothers lose clarity, patience and self-trust — and the relationship with the partner, the other children, and their own faith silently pays the price.
It is not weakness, it is physiology. And there is a real path forward — without rigid methods and without promising what cannot be promised.
journeyBody: I listen first. I don't show up with a ready-made plan for your baby — I show up with questions to understand the bond, your child's temperament, and the realistic rhythm of your home.
Then we leave with an honest plan: what you do tonight, what we adjust next week, what we revisit together. Ongoing support, not a one-time consult.
forWhomItems: Mothers of babies between 4 and 18 months
Mothers tired of method X, method Y, formulas that ignore who your child actually is
Mothers who want to sleep again without giving up who they are
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from the Sleep Consulting page — I'd like to talk about my baby's sleep

cards.description: Mentorship for mothers in transition: first child, second child, identity after birth, marriage after baby. Honest talk, practical tools, gentle support.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from your site — I'd like to know more about Real Motherhood Mentorship

detail.intro: Motherhood is not a miracle that happens to you. It is a crossing you cross — and almost no one is honest about what it's like from the inside. Identity that comes apart. Marriage that shifts. Body that returns different. Career on pause. And the silent question: "am I still me?".
whyBody: When motherhood is lived alone, it becomes survival. And mothers who survive for too long eventually collapse — into exhaustion, into anger, into distance.
It is not moral weakness. It is the absence of company. A mother needs another mother who has been through it, who has cried in the shower, who has come back to herself.
journeyBody: We talk. Really talk. This isn't spreadsheet coaching, it's woman-to-woman mentorship. I have been through the first child, the second, the marriage that needed to be relearned, the slow return to identity.
I listen, I ask questions back, I give you concrete tools. And I come back next week, and the week after, until you no longer need me.
forWhomItems: First-time mothers feeling lost in the middle of the obvious
Second-child mothers who feel they are starting from zero
Mothers who lost themselves in the work of raising people
Mothers who want to be whole — not only mothers
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from the Real Motherhood Mentorship page — I'd like to talk

cards.description: For mothers who feel the call to homeschool but don't know where to start. Routine, materials, a simple curriculum and the courage for the first week — and all the weeks after.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from your site — I'd like to know more about Homeschool Mentorship

detail.intro: You feel the call to educate at home. You've felt it since before the first child, or you started feeling it now that they're school age. But you don't know where to start — and every time you ask, someone scares you with paperwork, curriculum, isolation, or lack of time.
whyBody: Homeschooling is not a rejection of the world. It is a deep yes to time — the time to learn at the child's own pace, the time to build a bond while teaching, the time to pass on values that don't fit into a school schedule.
I have homeschooled my two daughters from day one, and it is the decision that has most redesigned our family. It is not easy. It is worth every hour.
journeyBody: I don't hand you a ready-made curriculum, because every home is different. I help you design yours: what values to pass on, what subjects to include, what routine fits your reality, what resources to use (free and paid).
Continuous mentorship, not a one-off class — so you have someone in the first week, the fifth, and the twentieth when the excitement fades and the method has to carry the house.
forWhomItems: Mothers considering homeschool for the first time
Families who have started but feel lost
Mothers already homeschooling who feel alone in the journey
Christian families wanting to align faith and education inside their own home
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from the Homeschool Mentorship page — I'd like to talk about educating at home

cards.description: Method of Intentional Traditional Education. Parental education that recovers clear limits, family hierarchy and character formation — with the intentionality modern parenting forgot.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from your site — I'd like to know more about METI Mentorship (Parental Education)

detail.intro: METI is the Method of Intentional Traditional Education — a way of raising children that recovers what modern parenting has forgotten (clear limits, family hierarchy, character formation, real family life) without falling into rigidity without purpose. It is parenting as before — but choosing every gesture.
whyBody: Most families today raise by improvisation. They react to children's behavior, copy what they saw in their own homes, outsource to school or screen. The result is a generation of exhausted parents and children without direction.
Not because parents don't love — but because no one taught them that raising is a vocation that has method. Tradition is not blind repetition; it is tested wisdom we choose to honor with intention.
journeyBody: In METI mentorship we work the pillars of the method applied to your specific family: how to establish limits without emotional punishment, how to build family liturgies (meals, prayer, rest), how to return authority to its place without becoming authoritarian, how to raise for character, not performance.
Regular meetings, continuous adjustments, real-life accompaniment of your actual home — not loose theory.
forWhomItems: Parents who feel raising children has become 24-hour negotiation
Families wanting to return to the intentionality they saw in their grandparents, adapted to today
Couples who want to align how they raise before the children grow up picking sides
Families of faith who want education to reflect what they believe, lived at home every day
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nagila, I came from the METI Mentorship page — I'd like to talk about parental education
